description: `getSnapshot` returns the full subtree for the requested root key rather than a partial fragment.
A snapshot may be empty when the root key exists but currently has no child value nodes.
`setSnapshot` replaces the full authoritative subtree baseline for the requested root key.
The current snapshot is replaced in place for sync purposes rather than requiring historical snapshot retention in the protocol.
After `setSnapshot` succeeds the server emits `snapshot-replaced` for that root key.
